content.text: Now I'll make the changes. First, the module: the pusher comment, the push invocation, and the stale `--mirror` mention in the hook.

input.old_string:
# Best-effort push replication. The pushes run detached (setsid) so a dead
  # mirror can only ever cost a log line — never block or fail the primary
  # push. `--mirror` propagates deletions, the correct semantic for a mirror.
  mirrorPusher =
    name: mirrors:
    pkgs.writeShellScript "valley-mirror-push-${name}" ''
      for url in ${lib.escapeShellArgs mirrors}; do
        if ${pkgs.git}/bin/git push --mirror "$url" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
input.new_string:
# Best-effort push replication. The pushes run detached (setsid) so a dead
  # mirror can only ever cost a log line — never block or fail the primary
  # push. Explicit refspecs with --prune force-update every branch and tag
  # and propagate their deletions — the same deletion semantics --mirror
  # gives for heads and tags. --mirror itself is rejected: it also tries to
  # delete remote-only namespaces, and on GitHub the read-only refs/pull/*
  # makes that fail every push, masking real replication failures.
  mirrorPusher =
    name: mirrors:
    pkgs.writeShellScript "valley-mirror-push-${name}" ''
      for url in ${lib.escapeShellArgs mirrors}; do
        if ${pkgs.git}/bin/git push --prune "$url" '+refs/heads/*:refs/heads/*' '+refs/tags/*:refs/tags/*' >/dev/null 2>&1; then

content.text: Now the schema comment, which documents the mechanism as `git push --mirror`:

content.content: The file /tmp/klaus-sessions/the-valley/20260713-0946-ee79b848/schema/valley.cue has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: -  // Push-mirror URLs. Every push to the primary is replicated to each
-  // URL via `git push --mirror`, which propagates deletions — the
-  // correct semantic for a mirror. Replication is best-effort: a dead
-  // mirror never rejects the primary push. Credentials are the host's
-  // concern (the installer documents how); they are not declared here.
+  // Push-mirror URLs. Every push to the primary replicates all branches
+  // and tags to each URL, force-updating and propagating deletions —
+  // the correct semantic for a mirror. Remote-only namespaces (e.g.
+  // GitHub's refs/pull/*) are never touched. Replication is best-effort:
+  // a dead mirror never rejects the primary push. Credentials are the
+  // host's concern (the installer documents how); they are not declared

input.old_string:
# The repo init list and mirror hook must be generated from
                  # the CUE export, and the git user's sshd Match block must
                  # be terminated so it cannot scope later config.
                  grep -q "the-valley" "$initScriptPath"
                  grep -q "valley-mirrors" "$initScriptPath"
                  grep -q "Match All" "$sshdConfigPath"
input.new_string:
# The repo init list and mirror hook must be generated from
                  # the CUE export, and the git user's sshd Match block must
                  # be terminated so it cannot scope later config.
                  grep -q "the-valley" "$initScriptPath"
                  grep -q "valley-mirrors" "$initScriptPath"
                  grep -q "Match All" "$sshdConfigPath"

                  # The mirror push must use explicit head/tag refspecs with
                  # --prune, never --mirror: --mirror also deletes remote-only
                  # refs, and GitHub's read-only refs/pull/* fails every such
                  # push. Follow the hook chain from the init script to the
                  # rendered push script and pin the invocation there.
                  mirrorHook="$(grep -o '/nix/store/[^ ]*-valley-mirrors-[^ ]*' "$initScriptPath" | head -n1)"
                  mirrorPush="$(grep -o '/nix/store/[^ ]*-valley-mirror-push-[^ ]*' "$mirrorHook" | head -n1)"
                  grep -q -- 'push --prune' "$mirrorPush"
                  grep -qF -- '+refs/heads/*:refs/heads/*' "$mirrorPush"
                  grep -qF -- '+refs/tags/*:refs/tags/*' "$mirrorPush"
                  if grep -q -- '--mirror' "$mirrorPush"; then
                    echo "module-eval: mirror push regressed to --mirror" >&2
                    exit 1
                  fi

                  # The mirror push must use explicit head/tag refspecs with

lines: +                  # The mirror push must use explicit head/tag refspecs with
+                  # refs, and GitHub's read-only refs/pull/* fails every such
+                  # push. Follow the hook chain from the init script to the
+                  # rendered push script and pin the invocation there.
# The rendered restic units must back up the data directory

content.text: Now let me check the README for any description of the mirror mechanism, and run `nix flake check`:

input.description: Check README for mirror mechanism references

content.content: 35:Phase 0 in progress. The repo ships its first artifacts: the CUE schema defining what a valley host declares — its projects, their push mirrors, and the durability policy for its data ([`schema/valley.cue`](./schema/valley.cue), with an example in [`examples/host.cue`](./examples/host.cue)) — and a NixOS module ([`flake.nix`](./flake.nix)) that installs a valley host from that declaration. The plan-of-record — incremental, MVP-first, validation-gated — is [`design/roadmap.md`](./design/roadmap.md).

tool_use_result.stdout: 35:Phase 0 in progress. The repo ships its first artifacts: the CUE schema defining what a valley host declares — its projects, their push mirrors, and the durability policy for its data ([`schema/valley.cue`](./schema/valley.cue), with an example in [`examples/host.cue`](./examples/host.cue)) — and a NixOS module ([`flake.nix`](./flake.nix)) that installs a valley host from that declaration. The plan-of-record — incremental, MVP-first, validation-gated — is [`design/roadmap.md`](./design/roadmap.md).

input.command:
git add flake.nix nix/valley-host.nix schema/valley.cue && git commit -m "Mirror push: explicit head/tag refspecs + --prune instead of --mirror

Against GitHub mirrors, --mirror also tries to delete the read-only
refs/pull/* namespace; GitHub rejects those deletions, so every mirror
push exits nonzero and the hook logs FAILED even when heads and tags
replicated fine. Explicit refspecs with --prune keep the semantics that
matter (force-update all branches and tags, propagate their deletions)
while never touching remote-only namespaces.

The module-eval check now follows the hook chain to the rendered push
script and pins the refspecs, so a regression to --mirror fails the
flake check.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>" && git push -u origin HEAD 2>&1 | tail -3

input.command:
gh pr create --reviewer patflynn --title "Mirror push: explicit head/tag refspecs + --prune instead of --mirror" --body "## Why

Against a GitHub mirror, \`git push --mirror\` also tries to delete the remote's read-only \`refs/pull/*\` namespace, and GitHub rejects those deletions — so every mirror push exits nonzero and the hook logs \`push to \$url FAILED\` even when all heads and tags replicated fine. That permanent noise makes replication health illegible exactly when we start relying on it.

## What

The per-project pusher now runs \`git push --prune \"\$url\" '+refs/heads/*:refs/heads/*' '+refs/tags/*:refs/tags/*'\`: same semantics where they matter — force-updates all branches and tags and propagates their deletions — but never touches \`refs/pull\` or any other remote-only namespace. The setsid best-effort structure and logging are unchanged; the module and schema comments now explain why \`--mirror\` is rejected.

The \`module-eval\` check previously only pinned \`valley-mirrors\` in the init script; it now follows the hook chain to the rendered push script and pins \`--prune\` plus both refspecs (and rejects \`--mirror\`), verified to fail on a regression. \`nix flake check\` and \`klaus _pre-review\` pass.

## Note

This is deliberately the **last the-valley change made through GitHub PR flow** — the repo migrates to its own valley host (S1 direct-push mode) immediately after, with GitHub demoted to a push mirror fed by this very mechanism.

Run: 20260713-0946-ee79b848" 2>&1 | tail -2

content.text:
Done — PR #18 is open: https://github.com/gunk-dev/the-valley/pull/18

**What changed:**

- **`nix/valley-host.nix`** — `mirrorPusher` now runs `git push --prune "$url" '+refs/heads/*:refs/heads/*' '+refs/tags/*:refs/tags/*'` instead of `git push --mirror`. This keeps the deletion semantics that matter (force-updates all branches and tags, `--prune` propagates their deletions) while never touching GitHub's read-only `refs/pull/*`, which was rejecting every `--mirror` push and logging false `FAILED` lines. The comment explains the refined choice, the stale `--mirror replicates everything` note in the hook was updated, and the setsid/logging structure is untouched.
- **`schema/valley.cue`** — the `mirrors` field doc no longer names `--mirror`; it now describes the semantics (replicate/force-update/delete heads and tags, never remote-only namespaces).
- **`flake.nix`** — the module-eval check only grepped `valley-mirrors` in the init script, so the push command text was unpinned. It now follows the hook chain (init script → `valley-mirrors-*` hook → `valley-mirror-push-*` script) using the same store-path-following technique the check already used for restic paths, and pins `push --prune` plus both refspecs, with an explicit failure if `--mirror` reappears.

**Verification:** `nix flake check` passes; I also ran a negative test — temporarily reverting the push to `--mirror` makes the module-eval check fail, so the pin genuinely guards against regression. `klaus _pre-review` reported no issues.

The PR body covers the refs/pull failure mode, the semantics comparison, and notes this is deliberately the last the-valley change through GitHub PR flow before the repo migrates to its own valley host. The "1 uncommitted change" warning from `gh` is just the pre-existing untracked `.claude/` directory, which I left alone.

result:
Done — PR #18 is open: https://github.com/gunk-dev/the-valley/pull/18

**What changed:**

- **`nix/valley-host.nix`** — `mirrorPusher` now runs `git push --prune "$url" '+refs/heads/*:refs/heads/*' '+refs/tags/*:refs/tags/*'` instead of `git push --mirror`. This keeps the deletion semantics that matter (force-updates all branches and tags, `--prune` propagates their deletions) while never touching GitHub's read-only `refs/pull/*`, which was rejecting every `--mirror` push and logging false `FAILED` lines. The comment explains the refined choice, the stale `--mirror replicates everything` note in the hook was updated, and the setsid/logging structure is untouched.
- **`schema/valley.cue`** — the `mirrors` field doc no longer names `--mirror`; it now describes the semantics (replicate/force-update/delete heads and tags, never remote-only namespaces).
- **`flake.nix`** — the module-eval check only grepped `valley-mirrors` in the init script, so the push command text was unpinned. It now follows the hook chain (init script → `valley-mirrors-*` hook → `valley-mirror-push-*` script) using the same store-path-following technique the check already used for restic paths, and pins `push --prune` plus both refspecs, with an explicit failure if `--mirror` reappears.

**Verification:** `nix flake check` passes; I also ran a negative test — temporarily reverting the push to `--mirror` makes the module-eval check fail, so the pin genuinely guards against regression. `klaus _pre-review` reported no issues.

The PR body covers the refs/pull failure mode, the semantics comparison, and notes this is deliberately the last the-valley change through GitHub PR flow before the repo migrates to its own valley host. The "1 uncommitted change" warning from `gh` is just the pre-existing untracked `.claude/` directory, which I left alone.
